title: Starlink 10-44 Delayed: B1067's Record 34th Flight Postponed
detail: SpaceX's planned launch of Starlink Group 10-44 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station was delayed from its targeted March 27 window. The mission was set to use booster B1067 on its 34th flight, which would set a new record for the most flights by a single Falcon 9 first-stage booster. B1067 has been in service since June 2021, having first flown on the CRS-22 cargo resupply mission to the International Space Station. The mission was scheduled to deploy 29 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites to orbital Shell 10 with recovery on drone ship 'Just Read the Instructions' in the Atlantic. The delay pushed the mission to late March or early April.
